---
title: Stock Report
date: today
date-format: long
published-title: produced
jupyter: python3
format: email
attachments:
  - data.csv
email-preview: true
---

## Report for GSPC

```{python}
#| echo: true

import pandas as pd
```

```{python}
#| eval: false
#| echo: false
# Cached data (gspc.csv) is used by default. To refresh it, set the `eval`
# option above to true to pull fresh data from Yahoo Finance, render once,
# then set it back to false.
import yfinance as yf
spx = yf.Ticker("^GSPC")
hist = spx.history("5y")
hist.to_csv("gspc.csv")
```

```{python}
hist = pd.read_csv("gspc.csv", index_col=0)
hist.index = pd.to_datetime(hist.index, utc=True)
hist['Change'] = hist['Close'].diff()

latest = hist.index[-1]
days_90 = latest - pd.to_timedelta("90D")

prices_1d  = hist[latest:]
prices_90d = hist[days_90:]
prices_rolling = hist.asfreq('D').rolling(window=52*7, min_periods=1)

data=[
    [
        prices_1d['High'].values[0],
        prices_1d['Low'].values[0],
        prices_1d['Volume'].values[0].round(),
    ],
    [
        prices_rolling.max()[latest:]['High'].values[0],
        prices_rolling.min()[latest:]['Low'].values[0],
        prices_rolling.mean()[latest:]['Volume'].values[0].round(),
    ]
]

df = pd.DataFrame(
    data,
    columns=['High', 'Low', 'Volume'],
    index=['Most Recent Trading Day', '52-Week'])
df
```

### Price History

The price history is read from a cached `gspc.csv` file, which can be refreshed from Yahoo Finance.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-history
#| fig-cap: "GSPC price history"

_ = hist['Close'].plot(grid=True)
```

## Raw Data

```{python}
#| echo: false

hist
```

## Downloadable Data

This report also writes the recent data to a CSV file and includes it as a downloadable attachment, so stakeholders can grab the underlying numbers.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| include: false

prices_90d.to_csv("data.csv")
```

[Link to CSV](data.csv)

## Email

This report also produces an email that is sent to key stakeholders with summary
information.

::: {.email}

::: {.subject}
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

closes = prices_90d['Close']
change = closes.iloc[-1] - closes.iloc[-2]
abschange = abs(change)
updown = "up"
if change < 0:
    updown = "down"
print(f"GSPC is {updown} by {abschange:.2f} points ({latest:%B %d, %Y})")
```
:::

Hello Team,

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

print(f"Here are the latest **GSPC** index levels as of {latest:%B %d, %Y}:")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false

prices_90d.tail(5)[['Open', 'Close', 'Change', 'Volume']]
```

The historical trend is shown below:

```{python}
#| echo: false

_ = prices_90d['Close'].plot(grid=True)
```

Let me know if you have any questions.

Best,

Team Lead

:::
